channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Filing in Trump case details remarkable schism with Pence over rejecting 2020 election loss
item.description: Mike Pence is no longer standing beside Donald Trump, and has refused to endorse the Republican nominee’s bid to return to the White House.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Mascaro, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Californians' crime concerns put pressure on criminal justice reform and progressive DAs
item.description: Ten years ago, 3.7 million Californians voted for a landmark measure that made many nonviolent crimes misdemeanors instead of felonies.
item.dc:creator: Jaimie Ding And Trân Nguyễn, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pressure building on Mayor Eric Adams following federal indictment
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Pressure continues to build on Mayor Eric Adams -- with more federal corruption charges possible. On Thursday, the mayor seems to have forced out his schools chancellor sooner than expected, with the governor telling him to make sure he keeps the city running smoothly.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Massachusetts governor implements new gun law weeks ahead of schedule
item.description: Massachusetts Gov. Maura Healey was ahead of schedule Wednesday, when she implemented sweeping gun reform expected to go into law later in October.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gallego rakes in close to $22M in third quarter for Arizona Senate race
item.description: Rep. Ruben Gallego (D-Ariz.) raised close to $22 million in the third quarter of fundraising for this year in the Arizona Senate race, his campaign announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democrat Mondaire Jones announces nearly $3M fundraising haul in NY House race
item.description: Former Rep. Mondaire Jones’s (D-N.Y.) campaign announced Thursday that the candidate had raked in $2.9 million in the third quarter of fundraising this year, a number that was first shared with The Hill. Jones’s campaign said it ended the quarter with more than $2.3 million in the bank.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minneapolis mayor, City Council clash on homeless shelter funding
item.description: The Minneapolis City Council is launching an inquiry into the mayor’s financial reporting practices following a dispute over funding for a homeless shelter.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Trump's campaign says he raised $160 million in September, ended month with $283 million banked
item.description: Former President Donald Trump raised $160 million for his campaign in September and entered October with $283 million in the bank for the campaign's final sprint, his aides announced.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Valadao, Salas critique each other's records during CA-22 debate
item.description: BAKERSFIELD, Calif. (KGET) -- The hour-long debate featured a deep dive into local, state and federal issues, as well as critiques on each other's records as lawmakers. One of the most highly-contested races nationwide, CA-22 is a difficult-to-predict toss-up.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Huh, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump targets Biden, Harris over federal response to hurricane: 'Incompetently managed'
item.description: As President Biden spends a second straight day in the Southeast surveying storm damage from Hurricane Helene, former President Trump blasts President Biden and Vice President Kamala Harris over the federal government's relief efforts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Politically charged rumors and conspiracy theories about Helene flourish on X
item.description: False claims about the hurricane and the government’s response got millions of views on social media. Emergency management researchers say it makes it harder for useful information to surface.
item.dc:creator: Huo Jingnan, MPR News

item.title: Trump on Springfield Haitian migrants: 'They have to be removed'
item.description: The former president spoke to NewsNation's Ali Bradley about his plans regarding the Haitian migrants living in Springfield, Ohio.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Trump calls Jack Smith filing 'pure election interference'
item.description: The former president spoke exclusively to NewsNation about the unsealing of documents related to his election interference case.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Are Nevada voters swayed by promises of 'no tax on tips'?
item.description: Reporter John Langeler from KLAS in Las Vegas joins "The Hill" to discuss whether Nevada voters are swayed by proposals to eliminate federal taxes on tips.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Watch: FOX59/CBS4 Indiana Governor Debate
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS -- For the first time this election year, the two major party candidates are having the opportunity to participate in a televised debate surrounding issues that are important for Indiana voters. U.S. Senator Mike Braun, R-Ind.
item.dc:creator: David Gay, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota abortion law signed by Walz scrutinized during VP debate
item.description: Internet searches about Minnesota’s abortion law — the first state to codify access after the Supreme Court overturned Roe v. Wade — were high during Tuesday night’s vice presidential debate.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: State of Democra-Z: Takeaways from the vice presidential debate
item.description: The vice presidential debate is not expected to push many voters to one side or another, but it offered a closer look at the men who could be second in command. We check in with two Gen-Z voters about what stood out to them.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Alanna Elder, MPR News

item.title: Memorable moments from the vice presidential debate
item.description: Vice presidential nominees Sen. JD Vance (R-Ohio) and Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D) clashed Tuesday in what is likely to be the last debate of this presidential election season.
item.dc:creator: Sarakshi Rai, WGN 9

item.title: Poll: Who won the Walz-Vance VP Debate?
item.description: (NEXSTAR) - Political scientists will tell you that vice presidential nominees rarely swing the election in favor of the candidate at the top of the ticket. But poll after poll shows this election cycle coming down to a percentage point across a handful of key swing states.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, Fox 5

item.title: Voters react to Gov Tim Walz dodging Tiananmen Square question: 'I'm a knucklehead at times'
item.description: A focus group of voters were skeptical of Gov. Tim Walz's explanation for falsely claiming to have been in Hong Kong during the Tiananmen Square massacre.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: In VP debate, Walz acknowledges he wasn’t in China during Tiananmen uprising
item.description: APM Reports, the investigations and documentaries unit of MPR News, first reported that Walz’s claim during a 2014 congressional hearing on the 25th anniversary of the massacre didn’t square up with media accounts in 1989.
item.dc:creator: Tom Scheck, Curtis Gilbert, Caspar von Au, and Forest Hunt, MPR News

item.title: Vance on carbon emissions and climate change: 'Let's just say that's true'
item.description: “One of the things that I've noticed some of our Democratic friends talking a lot about is a concern about carbon emissions — this idea that carbon emissions drive all the climate change,” Vance said.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, NewsNation

item.title: NY race for US Senate: Gillibrand talks Adams indictment, term limits, economy
item.description: Come Nov. 5, the 15-year Democratic senator will defend her seat against Republican challenger Mike Sapraicone.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, NewsNation

item.title: NY race for US Senate: Sapraicone on the border, abortion, gun control
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – With a Senate seat up for grabs, Republican Mike Sapraicone joined PIX11 News on Tuesday night to discuss gun control, abortion, crime, and other key issues. The retired NYPD detective will take on incumbent Democratic Sen. Kirsten Gillibrand in the Nov. 5 general election.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Are convicted felons allowed to vote in Illinois?
item.description: With just five weeks until the 2024 presidential election, most U.S. citizens over the age of 18 are eligible to vote as long as they are registered. However, for some individuals, the right to vote may be more complicated due to a criminal conviction.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, WGN 9

item.title: U.S. Fish and Wildlife signs Tamarac co-stewardship agreement with White Earth
item.description: The U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service and the White Earth Nation have signed a co-stewardship agreement for the Tamarac National Wildlife Refuge in northwestern Minnesota. The agreement will not change public access to the refuge, but will give tribal resource managers more input in refuge management.
item.dc:creator: Dan Gunderson, MPR News

item.title: Gillibrand vs. Sapraicone: The Race for US Senate forum
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Democratic Sen. Kirsten Gillibrand and Republican candidate Mike Sapraicone faced off in a Senate forum that aired on PIX11 News Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Israel begins ground offensive in Lebanon
item.description: The Israeli military pressed its ground incursion into southern Lebanon on Tuesday, calling the operations “limited incursions” that are targeting Hezbollah militants.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin, Kat Lonsdorf, and Miguel Macias, MPR News

item.title: Vance and Walz are both veterans. Here's what it means to have a vet VP
item.description: For the first time since 1996, both major parties have picked a veteran for vice president — a fact that some veterans' groups are hoping it leads to greater understanding of military and vet issues.
item.dc:creator: Quil Lawrence, MPR News

item.title: Walz, Vance split on abortion, border, economic policies in issues-driven debate
item.description: Questions around climate change, border concerns, abortion and the economy drove Tuesday night’s vice presidential faceoff. Vance and Walz remained largely cordial in the debate and steered away from personal attacks.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: A century of Jimmy Carter: Longest-living former president turns 100
item.description: In 1998, at the ripe age of 74, former President Carter put some of his thoughts about getting older to paper. "The virtues of aging," Carter wrote in a book that year, "include both the blessings that come to us as we grow older and what we have to offer that might be beneficial to others.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: Biden administration expands asylum restrictions at southern border
item.description: The Biden administration announced Monday it will expand its asylum restrictions at the U.S.-Mexico border, cementing tougher standards that will cut off access to protections for many migrants.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: What to know about the Walz-Vance debate
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D) and Sen. JD Vance (R-Ohio) are set to go head-to-head on Tuesday night in their first and likely only debate as vice presidential contenders.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, Fox 5

item.title: Trump suspect pleads not guilty in attempted assassination
item.description: The man accused of attempting to assassinate former President Trump at one of his Florida golf courses earlier this month has pleaded not guilty to five federal charges, according to The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Here are the rules for the Vance-Walz CBS debate
item.description: The debate, which will kick off at 9 p.m. EDT on Tuesday from the network’s New York office, will be moderated by CBS anchor Norah O’Donnell and "Face the Nation" host Margaret Brennan.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Here are the rules for the Vance-Walz debate
item.description: The debate, which will kick off at 9 p.m. EDT on Tuesday from the network’s New York office, will be moderated by CBS anchor Norah O’Donnell and "Face the Nation" host Margaret Brennan.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Oregon senator introduces bill to reform Supreme Court, add 6 more seats
item.description: Oregon Sen. Ron Wyden introduced bill on Thursday that would bring fundamental changes to the nation's high court.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Bourgeois, Fox 5
